questions.question: Explain the difference between perfect and imperfect information games with examples.
expectedPoints: Perfect information: all players have complete knowledge of game state (e.g., Chess, Checkers)
Imperfect information: players have hidden information (e.g., Poker, Bridge)
Impact on AI algorithms: perfect information allows complete game trees, imperfect requires probability and inference

questions.question: Describe the minimax algorithm with alpha-beta pruning. Include a simple example to demonstrate how pruning works.
expectedPoints: Minimax algorithm: recursive method for optimal move selection in zero-sum games
Alpha-beta pruning: optimization that eliminates branches that cannot influence final decision
Alpha: best value maximizer can guarantee, Beta: best value minimizer can guarantee
Pruning condition: when alpha ≥ beta, remaining branches can be pruned
Example: trace through a simple game tree showing which branches would be pruned
